personal_info.name: Anway Bose
personal_info.headline: Ph.D. Candidate in Electrical and Computer Engineering | Robotics & Distributed AI
personal_info.relocation: Open to relocation (San Francisco, New York, or any major tech hub)

summary: Robotics and Distributed AI researcher with Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering at Temple University. Specialized in hardware-validated embodied AI, privacy-preserving federated learning, and distributed control systems. Demonstrated expertise translating theoretical advances into production-ready systems: sim-to-real RL deployment, homomorphic encryption frameworks, Lyapunov-certified controllers, and battlefield-grade distributed AI testbeds for the U.S. Army. 10 peer-reviewed publications across 5 research domains. Proficient in PyTorch, ROS2, Isaac Sim, C++, and real-time embedded systems.
research_interests: Embodied AI and Sim-to-Real Transfer Learning
Reinforcement Learning with Formal Stability Guarantees

experience.organization: Computer Fusion Lab, Temple University & DEVCOM Analysis Center (U.S. Army Collaboration)
experience.description: Lead researcher on embodied AI, privacy-preserving federated learning, and distributed control. Translating theoretical advances into hardware-validated systems with physical robot deployment and production integration.
highlights: Sim-to-Real RL: Trained deep RL locomotion policies in Isaac Sim, transferred zero-shot to physical MiniPupper legged robot—stable on uneven terrain on first hardware run, no domain randomization tuning required.

Humanoid VLA Pipeline: Built autonomous ROS2 perception-to-actuation pipeline for DARwIn OP3 humanoid, integrating multi-agent LLM tool-use with constrained decoding. Failure rate on novel configurations reduced from 30% to under 8%.

Homomorphic Federated Learning: Developed first fully decentralized FL framework under CKKS encryption with zero plaintext gradient exposure and mathematically proven Byzantine-robust convergence (no prior literature).

Lyapunov-Certified RL Control: Derived descent conditions for RL controllers under function approximation—first formal stability certificate for RL-based prosthetic control, validated via hardware-in-the-loop on real systems.
ADMM Distributed Optimization: Convergence-guaranteed 3D spatial estimation across multi-sensor arrays with closed-form convergence rate bounds for nonconvex coupling at 50+ agent scale.

Wearable Biosensor Platform: Designed and deployed ROS2 multi-modal EMG + IMU sensor platform with firmware-level synchronization and real-time ML inference pipeline—running as live research infrastructure.
DEVCOM Secure AI Testbed: Designed from problem statement including threat model, TGDH key management, and CKKS encrypted aggregation for battlefield distributed AI (U.S. Army contract).

Bayesian Distributed Estimation: Proved posterior consistency under 30% adversarial data corruption with non-i.i.d. data, quantifying concentration rate as function of prior, sample size, and corruption fraction.

Robotics Course Design: Designed and taught graduate course on sim-to-real RL with all labs, derivations, and HIL protocols written from scratch; students achieved physical robot deployment as capstone.
Multi-Agent Power Grid Resilience: Federated RL for shipboard power system control with generalization from simulation to real-time deployment with no architecture change.

experience.description: Designed and implemented nonlinear model predictive control for Permanent Magnet Linear Synchronous Motors in semiconductor bonding hardware with production-grade deployment.
highlights: Designed nonlinear MPC for PMLSM in semiconductor bonding hardware—formulated optimization, frequency-domain stability analysis, implemented in production C++ on DSP at sub-millisecond latency.
Built HIL evaluation pipeline validated under production noise and latency—adopted as team standard benchmarking infrastructure.
Developed comprehensive Model Predictive Controller design for Ball Bonder machine PMLSM with real-time performance validation.

experience.description: Developed advanced estimation algorithms for wheeled robots, SLAM, and multi-agent localization with convergence guarantees.
highlights: Developed GP-based model-free kinematic calibration for wheeled robots—fused odometry and IMU data without closed-form model, validated on 3 physical platforms, diagnosed and resolved systematic IMU calibration bias.
Implemented Gauss-Newton, LM, EM, EKF estimators for SLAM and localization with successive convex approximation solvers scaling to 50+ agents with convergence guarantees.
Developed scalable algorithms for multi-agent estimation with theoretical convergence proofs.

experience.description: Taught and mentored graduate students in control theory, embedded systems, and engineering fundamentals.
highlights: Conducted recitations and developed problem sets for graduate-level courses.
Mentored graduate students in control theory and optimization.
Courses: Control Systems, Circuits and Electronics, Digital Circuit Design, Engineering Analysis, Engineering Programming
